title: cissus subciliata
intro: Cissus subciliata, also known as the climbing vine of Africa, is a type of plant belonging to the grape family that is native to tropical Africa and Madagascar. This plant has been traditionally used in African medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Cissus subciliata has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing joint pain and inflammation, improving bone density, and aiding in weight loss. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, capsules, and infusions, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
